diagnosis:
The strategy detects classic price/RSI divergence over a rolling `divergence_lookback` window split into two equal halves. For bullish divergence it compares minimum prices and RSI values in the first half vs the second half; for bearish divergence it compares maximums. It requires RSI to be below `rsi_extreme_low` (35) for bullish and above `rsi_extreme_high` (65) for bearish signals. An ADX cap (`adx_max` = 35) forces it away from strong trends, and an optional volume-decline filter exists. Stop/take-profit are ATR-based (2.5x / 3.5x). Time stop at 36 hours.

The strategy DOES emit short signals: the bearish-divergence branch generates `Signal(side="short", ...)`, including a flipped stop_loss (close + ATR * mult) and take_profit (close - ATR * mult). This branch is active and must be dropped for Phase-3 compliance.

The strategy was explicitly listed in Round 1 of the STRATEGY_REGISTRY pruning notes in auto_evolve.py with 0% positive fitness across 29,000 evaluations. It is not in the current active STRATEGY_REGISTRY. It therefore has NO demonstrated viability in the existing evolutionary framework.

Likely edge: RSI bullish divergence can capture genuine mean-reversion bounces from oversold levels in ranging/lateral markets, but in crypto's dominant trending-up environment the signal fires rarely and often too early. The half-window min/max comparison is a very crude approximation of swing divergence — it catches coincidental coincidences in noisy data at least as often as true structural divergence. The bearish branch is irrelevant for long-only and further pollutes the signal space. The regime gate (lateral, mean_reversion, trend) is broad enough to let almost anything through.

Weakness: The divergence detection (split-window min/max) does not identify actual price swing pivots. It is equivalent to comparing averages of two halves, making it sensitive to any transient dip. Very low true-positive rate in trending markets. RSI thresholds of 35/65 are loose for crypto (RSI can stay near 70 for weeks in strong uptrends). The 0% positive fitness in 29K evolutionary evals is the decisive evidence that even generous parameter sweeps cannot find a profitable regime.
long_only_hypothesis: In ranging or early-recovery crypto regimes, price sometimes makes a lower low while RSI makes a higher low, signalling exhaustion of selling pressure. If this divergence is detected at a confirmed swing low (not just a half-window minimum artifact), near strong support (BMSB, VWAP, Bollinger lower band), with volume contraction during the re-test leg, the setup can produce a mean-reversion bounce that justifies going long spot with a tight ATR stop. The long-only version drops the bearish branch entirely and adds regime, support-proximity, and volume-contraction filters to reduce false positives, making it long-or-flat only. The core bet is: oversold RSI divergence + volume dry-up + structural support = exhausted sellers; enter long, target prior swing high or 3-4x ATR, exit on time stop if the bounce fails.
market_techniques: Split-window RSI divergence (bullish branch only, drop bearish entirely)
ADX cap to avoid strong-trend false signals (mean-reversion context)
Volume contraction confirmation on the re-test leg (volume_ratio < threshold signals seller exhaustion)
Bollinger Band lower-band proximity filter (price near or below lower band adds structural support context)
BMSB (Bull Market Support Band) as regime gate — only trade longs when price is above or re-testing the BMSB
VWAP distance filter — entries only when price is near or below VWAP_20 to avoid chasing
Pressure imbalance as entry timing — buying_pressure rising at divergence confirmation
OBV divergence cross-check — OBV should NOT be making a new low to confirm divergence is real
Time-based exit (time_stop_hours) as primary exit when take-profit not reached
useful_features: momentum: rsi_14 — primary divergence oscillator, current-bar level gate
momentum: rsi_7 — faster RSI for entry timing refinement after divergence detection
trend: adx_14 — cap to ensure mean-reversion context; high ADX = skip
volatility: atr_14 — stop and take-profit distance denominator
volatility: atr_7 — shorter-window ATR for tighter stop in low-vol regimes
volatility: bb_pct_b — proximity to Bollinger lower band as support confirmation
volatility: bb_bandwidth — low bandwidth = compression before potential bounce
volatility: gk_vol — regime filter; extremely high GK vol = avoid (gap risk)
volume: volume_ratio — confirm volume contraction on re-test (require_volume_decline filter)
volume: obv_divergence — OBV vs OBV_SMA; positive divergence supports bullish price divergence
volume: volume_zscore — spike detector; avoid entering on volume spike re-tests
trend: bmsb_bullish — boolean flag; long-only entries only when above BMSB or re-testing it
mean-reversion: dist_from_low_20 — how far price is from 20-bar low; low distance = oversold context
mean-reversion: ret_zscore_20 — z-score of recent returns; extreme negative z-score confirms oversold
microstructure: pressure_imbalance — buying_pressure - selling_pressure; should turn positive at divergence confirmation
microstructure: buying_pressure — close relative to bar range; rising at signal bar is confirming
price: vwap_20 — support/resistance context; entries below VWAP are better risk-adjusted
market regime: regime string — gate on lateral / mean_reversion only (drop trend from allowed_regimes to reduce false breakout entries)

genome_params.name: divergence_lookback
genome_params.note: Number of bars over which split-window divergence is measured. Shorter = more frequent but noisier signals; longer = rarer but more confirmed divergences.

genome_params.name: rsi_extreme_low
genome_params.note: RSI must be below this threshold for a bullish-divergence long entry. Tighter (25) = fewer but more oversold signals; looser (45) = more frequent.

genome_params.name: min_price_delta_pct
genome_params.note: Minimum percentage price decline from first-half min to second-half min to qualify as a lower low. Prevents triggering on negligible moves.

genome_params.name: min_rsi_delta
genome_params.note: Minimum RSI improvement from first-half min to second-half min to qualify divergence. Prevents weak divergence signals.

genome_params.name: adx_max
genome_params.note: Maximum ADX allowed for entry. Low values enforce mean-reversion context; high values allow entries in mild trends.

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for stop-loss distance. Must be wide enough to survive divergence noise but not so wide it destroys risk-reward.

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiplier for take-profit target. Should exceed stop multiplier to keep R:R positive.

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Maximum hours to hold if neither stop nor take-profit is hit. Divergence bounces typically resolve within 1-3 days; longer = more drift risk.

genome_params.name: require_volume_decline
genome_params.note: Toggle volume-contraction confirmation (volume_ratio below volume_decline_ratio). When True, filters signals where re-test volume is not drying up.

genome_params.name: volume_decline_ratio
genome_params.note: Threshold for volume_ratio below which volume is considered contracting. Only active when require_volume_decline=True.

genome_params.name: require_obv_confirm
genome_params.note: NEW: require OBV divergence (obv_divergence > 0) to co-confirm bullish price/RSI divergence. Reduces false positives.

genome_params.name: require_bmsb
genome_params.note: NEW: gate entries on bmsb_bullish flag (price above both BMSB SMA and EMA). Only enter longs in macro bull-market context.

genome_params.name: bb_pct_b_max
genome_params.note: NEW: maximum Bollinger bb_pct_b allowed at entry. Values near 0 mean price is near lower band, adding support-proximity confirmation.

do_not_optimize: rsi_period — fixing at 14 is standard; sweeping it creates look-ahead bias in divergence window construction since the same bars build both RSI and the divergence window
allowed_regimes — sweeping the regime set produces severe regime-label overfitting across the limited history; fix at lateral + mean_reversion only for long-only
require_regime — always True; turning it off defeats the strategy's only meaningful filter against trending false signals
divergence_lookback combined with rsi_period in the same sweep — these two parameters interact multiplicatively in the warmup skip, so sweeping both simultaneously vastly overfits the lookback space

risk_management.stop: ATR-based hard stop: close - atr_14 * stop_loss_atr_mult (genome range 1.5-4.0x). Stop is set at signal time and checked against bar low each subsequent bar. This is already implemented correctly in backtester._settle via position.stop_loss.
risk_management.take_profit: ATR-based take-profit: close + atr_14 * take_profit_atr_mult (genome range 2.0-6.0x). Must exceed stop multiplier. Fixed at signal time and checked against bar high.
risk_management.trailing: No trailing stop initially — divergence bounces have a discrete target (prior swing high). Optionally add a trailing stop that activates once unrealized gain exceeds 1.5x ATR, protecting a portion of gains against late reversals.
risk_management.vol_target: Volatility-scaled sizing (backtester sizing_method=volatility_scaled): target 15-20% annualized portfolio vol. When gk_vol is elevated (>80th percentile of trailing 90-day distribution), reduce size to 50% of normal. This prevents oversizing into volatile divergence setups that have wide stops.
risk_management.max_size: Hard cap at 25% of equity per trade (max_position_pct=0.25). Given the low win-rate expected from divergence patterns, full-kelly sizing is inappropriate. 25% cap combined with vol-targeting creates a practical ceiling.
risk_management.cooldown: Minimum 24-hour cooldown after any trade (win or loss) to prevent re-entering an identical divergence setup on the same price structure. Implement via risk_manager min_time_between_trades_hours=24.
risk_management.risk_off: Three-layer risk-off gate: (1) regime must be in [lateral, mean_reversion] — not trend (reduces breakout false signals); (2) bmsb_bullish=True gate prevents shorting against macro uptrend context when long-only (only enter if in macro bull context or re-testing BMSB); (3) circuit-breaker: if drawdown exceeds 15% from equity peak, halt new entries until recovery above 90% of peak — already supported by backtester/risk_manager circuit breaker logic.

metrics: CAGR — primary return metric; target >15% annualized on OOS 2024-2026 period
Sharpe ratio — target >0.8 on OOS; the strategy is low-frequency so a Sharpe below 0.6 is unacceptable given transaction costs
Sortino ratio — more relevant than Sharpe for long-only since upside variance is desirable; target >1.0
Max drawdown % — hard limit 20%; divergence strategies can string together many consecutive losses in trending markets
Calmar ratio (CAGR / MaxDD) — target >0.5; quantifies return-per-unit-drawdown
Hit rate (win%) — expected ~45-55% for a proper mean-reversion divergence; if below 40% in walk-forward OOS the signal has no edge
Average trade PnL net of costs — must be positive net of commission (0.04%) + slippage (5bps); divergence setups have wide ATR stops so the raw R:R must compensate
Turnover / trades per month — expect 2-6 trades/month given the rarity of valid divergence + all filters; below 2 means insufficient data for robust metrics
Market exposure % — time-in-market; divergence strategies should be <30% exposed (mostly in cash), which is correct for spot long-only
Cost sensitivity test — degrade commission by 2x and slippage by 2x; if Sharpe drops below 0 the strategy has no true edge beyond cost assumptions
Bootstrap/MC confidence intervals — run 500 equity-curve bootstrap samples on the OOS period; report 5th percentile Sharpe and maxDD to bound the luck component
Per-year Sharpe stability — decompose OOS into 2024 and 2025 annual Sharpes; a strategy that is positive in one year and negative in the other is not stable enough to deploy
OOS degradation % — walk-forward train vs OOS Sharpe degradation; target <30% degradation; the Round 1 pruning result (0% positive fitness) suggests severe degradation historically
sweep_changes: 1. DROP the entire bearish-divergence branch (lines 91-115 in rsi_divergence.py): delete the block that creates Signal(side='short',...). Replace with a comment: 'Bearish divergence branch removed — Phase-3 SPOT LONG-ONLY, short signals forbidden.'
2. RESTRICT allowed_regimes to ['lateral', 'mean_reversion'] only — remove 'trend' from PARAMS and hard-code it, do not sweep this set.

3. ADD require_obv_confirm parameter to PARAMS (default False) and implement: if require_obv_confirm and current.get('obv_divergence', 0) <= 0: return None. This adds OBV co-confirmation as a genome-sweepable filter.
4. ADD require_bmsb parameter to PARAMS (default False) and implement: if require_bmsb and not current.get('bmsb_bullish', True): return None. Macro long-only context gate.

5. ADD bb_pct_b_max parameter to PARAMS (default 1.0, i.e., inactive) and implement: if current.get('bb_pct_b', 0.5) > bb_pct_b_max: return None. Forces entries near lower Bollinger band for support-proximity confirmation.

6. ADD rsi_divergence entry to STRATEGY_REGISTRY in auto_evolve.py with the genome param_space defined above (divergence_lookback, rsi_extreme_low, min_price_delta_pct, min_rsi_delta, adx_max, stop_loss_atr_mult, take_profit_atr_mult, time_stop_hours, require_volume_decline, volume_decline_ratio, require_obv_confirm, require_bmsb, bb_pct_b_max).

7. FIX strength calculation: the current `min(abs(rsi_delta) / 20, 1.0)` is reasonable but should also factor in bb_pct_b (lower bb_pct_b = higher conviction). Replace with: strength = min((abs(rsi_delta) / 20) * (1.0 - max(0, current.get('bb_pct_b', 0.5) - 0.2)), 1.0). This reduces position size when price is not near the lower band.

8. CHANGE the divergence window split from symmetric half-halves to a 1/3 — 2/3 split so the 'first low' is drawn from an older period and the 'second low' is from a more recent compressed window (prices[-lookback//3:]), reducing contamination between the two periods.

9. ADD a minimum regime-stability check: require that the regime string has been 'lateral' or 'mean_reversion' for at least 3 consecutive bars before entry (a simple rolling check prevents regime transitions from triggering stale signals).

10. SET rsi_period to 14 in PARAMS and exclude it from the genome param_space entirely (see do_not_optimize). The rsi_col fallback to rsi_7 in the code should be removed and rsi_14 required explicitly.
methodological_risks: 1. Round-1 evolutionary null result: the strategy produced 0% positive fitness across 29,000 parameter evaluations. This is the strongest possible signal that no parameter combination within a generous search space produced a viable strategy. Re-adding it to the registry without substantial structural changes (not just parameter tightening) risks wasting evolutionary budget.

2. Split-window divergence is not swing-pivot divergence: comparing half-window min/max values is not the same as comparing confirmed price pivots. The method fires on any transient dip/peak, producing many spurious divergence detections. A proper pivot-based implementation (e.g., zigzag or local extrema detection) would be more robust but requires structural code changes.

3. Low trade count fragility: with all filters active (ADX cap, regime gate, RSI extreme, volume decline, BMSB, OBV), expected trade frequency is 2-4 per month at most. Across the 2-year OOS window this yields ~50-100 trades — too few for statistically robust Sharpe estimation. Bootstrap CI will be very wide.

4. Regime label dependency: the strategy gates on a regime string passed in from outside. The regime classifier's own accuracy and lag are not tested in isolation. A misclassified regime (e.g., labelling a trending market as 'lateral') will trigger entries into adverse conditions with no safety net.

5. ATR stop in mean-reversion context: a 1.5-4x ATR stop on a mean-reversion trade places the stop far below recent lows, meaning a normal counter-trend continuation takes out the stop before the bounce occurs. This is the core risk-reward problem for divergence strategies in volatile crypto assets.

6. Lookback window leakage risk at walk-forward boundaries: the divergence_lookback of 8-30 bars combined with the rsi_period of 14 creates a minimum warmup requirement. The embargo of 120 bars in WalkForwardValidator covers this, but the rsi EWM uses infinite history (min_periods=14 only sets the cutoff, not the actual lookback of the exponential kernel), meaning RSI values near train/test boundaries are still partially influenced by train data.

7. 1-bar execution lag is necessary but compounds slippage risk: divergence signals are inherently backward-looking and the bounce often happens within the same divergence bar. With a 1-bar entry lag (required by the backtester), the fill is at next bar's open — the best entry price is typically already gone. Slippage plus the 1-bar lag could eliminate the thin mean-reversion edge entirely.

8. Overfitting risk from boolean filter combinations: with 3 new boolean filters (require_obv_confirm, require_bmsb, require_volume_decline) plus bb_pct_b_max, the genome sweeps a space of 8 filter combinations on top of 9 continuous parameters. Each additional boolean doubles the combinatorial space and creates a curse-of-dimensionality problem for the genetic algorithm.
